title: The One With All the Wedding Dresses

lines.text: Joey's bedroom, he is asleep and snoring loudly. Chandler enters wondering who left their engine running.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you kidding me?! Joey. Joey! Joey! Joey! Joey! Joey! Joey! Joey! Joey! Joey!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (joining in, in his sleep) Joey. Joey. Joey. Joey! Joey!!
Chandler acts disgusted, but is happy that Joey has stopped snoring. However, just as he is about to leave, Joey starts snoring again. So to get him to stop, he slams the door shut, waking Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh. Oh, did-did-did I wake you?
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Chandler is getting another cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Gunther, can I get another cup of coffee, please? (Gunther starts to pour him another cup.) So uh, what do you do when you're not working here?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: You don't need to fill these silences.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, okay, thanks. (He goes back to the couch and rejoins Monica, Joey, and Phoebe.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, that's like your fourth cup of coffee!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I am drinking lots of cups of coffee because I'm exhausted! Because Joey started snoring!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's in a different room! He's really that loud?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (proudly) Oh, you should here me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's not something to be proud of, okay? You have to go to a sleep clinic!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, I told ya, I'm not going to any clinic! I don't have a problem, you're the one with the problem! You should go to a "Quit being a baby and leave me alone" clinic!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They don't have those.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, they do! Quit being a baby and leave me alone! There, you've just had your first class!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know I used to go out with this guy that was a really light sleeper, and whenever I started to snore, he would just roll me over...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhh, yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He would just roll me over and I would stop snoring.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Next time you snore, I'm rolling ya over!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I gotta do what I gotta do, you gotta do what you gotta do, you just do it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, all right!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!
Joey starts humming Here Come the Bride.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, the Olympics.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Have you guys picked a date yet?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no, not yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I still cannot believe you're engaged! (Ross looks at her) Just 'cause its happening so fast; not 'cause you're such a loser.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Thanks. Uh, has anyone seen Rach?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ugh, she's upstairs not doing the dishes! And I tell ya something! I'm not doing them this time! I don't care if those dishes sit in the sink until they're all covered with-I'll do them when I get home!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah-oh! Hey listen umm, Emily found this wedding dress in London...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Already?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but it didn't fit. Well, luckily there's a store here that has one left in her size, but I'm the groom, I'm not supposed to see the dress...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll pick it up for you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, she's got you running errands, y'know, picking up wedding dresses... (Laughs and makes like Indiana Jones and his whip) Wah-pah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's wah-pah?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, whipped! Wah-pah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's not whipped! Whipped is wh-tcssh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what I did. Wah-pah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You can't do anything!
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is not doing the dishes. She hears someone coming up the stairs and quickly puts down her magazine and pretends like she's actually doing the dishes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, Mon, I was just doing the dishes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! It's you. (She stops doing the dishes.) Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, do uh, do you have a minute?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, I was just about to take a break anyways, so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So listen uh, I know you and I haven't really had a chance to talk since uh, Emily and I decided to get married, and uh, I was just wondering how you were.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know if you were getting married I'd feel, kinda..... y'know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah. Definitely, well it definitely took me by surprise, but I'm okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, I just wanted to check.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that's sweet.
He goes over to hug her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're great. And I-I know someday this will happen for you too. You just hang in there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (breaking the hug) Uhh, hang in there?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no, I didn't mean, uh...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean maybe you didn't hear about a serious relationship called me and Joshua?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I thought you guys had just been on like four dates, I didn't realise that had become anything, yet.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, no-no-no, no-no-no, it has become, it has-yeah. Oh no, those were four great dates.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Oh, yeah. And I mean, the connection, I mean y'know, emotionally, mentally, physically...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, that's-that's-that's incredible.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know isn't it? It's like I'm right there with Joshua.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are right there with Emily. And it's y'know, it's kinda like.... it's a tie! Well, I gotta get, I gotta get back to the dishes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I gotta get to work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah? Fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know, y'know what would make me really happy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, no, what's that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: If like the four of us could all y'know, hang out together. Uh, in fact Emily's coming into town this weekend, why don't you say we all have dinner? Say, Sunday night?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That would be great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, all right, it's a date. (He leaves)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to the closed door) Hang in there. You hang in there. (Gives him the raspberry.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (coming back in) Did you say something?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, just singing. (Does a little song.)
Beatrice Bridal Shop, Monica and Phoebe are there to pick up Emily's dress.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! Ohh! Look at this one! It's so beautiful!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but y'know, about have of these are gonna end up getting divorced.

lines.speaker: The Saleslady
lines.text: May I help you ladies?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, yes, umm, I'm here to pick up a dress that you have on hold.

lines.speaker: The Saleslady
lines.text: Yes, what's the name, please?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Emily Waltham.

lines.speaker: The Saleslady
lines.text: Yes! I have it right here. (Phoebe and Monica both gasp at the dress.) Would you like to try it on Ms. Waltham?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) Okay.
[Time lapse. Monica is wearing the dress and starring at herself in the mirror.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're the most beautiful bride I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am, aren't I?

lines.speaker: The Saleslady
lines.text: Ms. Waltham?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: The Saleslady
lines.text: We're closing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. (Goes to take off the dress.)

lines.speaker: The Saleslady
lines.text: And could I get my ring back?
She disgustedly takes the ring off and gives it back.
Joey's bedroom, he's snoring again and Chandler is there to roll him over.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right buddy, time to roll over. (Rolls him over, and discovers a surprise) (Looking down) No-no! (Covers his eyes) No, no-n-n-n-no!! You are going to a clinic! You're going to a clinic, and a pyjama store!
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is doing the dishes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Does she use the cups? Yes! I believe she does. Does she use the plates? Yes! I believe she does. (Looks at the wedding dress and stops.)
[Time lapse, Monica is now wearing the dress while doing the dishes and is making like she is thanking her guests for coming to her wedding. Paging Dr. Crane. Dr. Fraiser Crane!]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. Thank you. Ohhh, thank you very much. Oh, thank you for coming. (There's a knock on the door.) Uh, just a second!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no, let me in!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Can you just hold on for one minute?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, you have to let me in right now!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you alone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right.
She goes over and lets Phoebe bounce in wearing her own wedding dress.
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is complaining about going to the clinic.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This sucks! I didn't know I had to stay up all night before I went to this stupid sleep clinic! I'm so tired!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, well...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, I hear that you and Joshua are going out to dinner with Ross and Emily, and I think that's, I think that's really cool.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, Rach, I think you're handling that really well.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Handling it? What do you mean, handling it? There's nothing to handle. Now, maybe I would have a problem with this if it wasn't for me and Joshua. Y'know, they're not gonna get married anyway!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on! They rushed into this thing so fast it's ridiculous! I mean, they're gonna be engaged for like what? A year? And somewhere along the way, one of them is gonna realise what they've done and they're call the whole thing off. I'm telling ya, you're gonna be dancing at my wedding before you're dancing at there's.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, I don't dance at weddings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because weddings are a great place to meet women, and when I dance, I look like this... (Starts to dancing really, really, really badly. Ross enters behind him and he stops.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, what are you guys doing four weeks from today?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I am... (Looks in his date book.) free!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great! Because Emily and I are getting married in a month!

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yep!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: In a month?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You mean, you mean 30 days?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: From now?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that's great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah, Emily always wanted to get married in this beautiful place that her parents got married, but it's going to be torn down, so... I mean, I-I know it's crazy, but everything up 'til now has been so crazy, and I don't know, this just feels right. Y'know?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (still looking in date book) Hey! That's the day after I stop menstruating! (They all look at him.) This isn't mine.
Commercial Break
Central Perk, Rachel is waiting impatiently for Joshua.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: (entering) Hey, Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: What's up? You're voice sounded all squeaky on the phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, nothing, I just wanted to see you. See you and hug you. (Hugs him) See you.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! (She sits down) Sit!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: (sitting) You okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm more than okay, I am really, really happy! Wanna know why?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Do I?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: 'Cause I am really happy about us. I think we are, I think we are so on the right track! Y'know? I mean, I think we are working, I think we are clicking. Y'know?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, sure-sure, yeah, we're-we're-we're-we're-we're clicking.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, y'know if-if there was just like one little area where I-that I think we need-we would need to work on; I-I would think it was we're just not crazy enough!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: I-I gotta say, I-I-I-I'm not too sure I agree with that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, yeah, right, y'know what? Yeah, you're right, I mean, we no, we have our fun. Yeah! But if (Grunts uncomprehensively)......I mean, I mean like craaaazy! Y'know? Okay, all right. This is gonna, this is gonna sound y'know, a little umm, hasty, but uh, just go with it. Umm. Ugh. What if we got married?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: What?! (Gunther is listening in.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know, I know, it's-it's so, it's so totally like, "Whoa! Can we do this?" Y'know, I mean, but I mean it just feels right! Don't you think? It does! I mean, it just feels right, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Wow! Uhh, Rachel uhh, you're a real special lady, but my divorce isn't final yet and, and, and we've been on four days, so I'm thinking "No, but thanks."

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: YOU IDIOT!!!!!
The Sleep Clinic, Joey is having trouble staying awake.

lines.speaker: Sleep Clinic Worker
lines.text: Your name, please?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Joey Tribbiani.

lines.speaker: Sleep Clinic Worker
lines.text: Um-hmm, and did you stay up all night in preparation for your sleep study. (Joey doesn't answer) Uh, sir? (Joey starts snoring)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (answering for him) Yes he did.

lines.speaker: Sleep Clinic Worker
lines.text: Alll right, we'll call you in a few minutes.
As she leaves, a beautiful woman enters and sits down across from the boys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (waking Joey) Hey, check out that girl! She is really hot!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sleepily) Yeah, she is. Wow! (Falls back asleep, loudly) How you doin'?
Chandler wakes him up, again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're coming on to the entire room! (He goes over to pick up a stack of magazines next to her, and to get her attention, he throws them back down.) I'm Chandler.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: I'm Marjorie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Marjorie
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mind if I...

lines.speaker: Marjorie
lines.text: No, please.
He sits down next to her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So uh, what are you in for?

lines.speaker: Marjorie
lines.text: I talk in my sleep.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What a coincidence, I listen in my sleep.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (asleep) So why don't you give me your number?
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Phoebe, still defying reality, are now throwing a bouquet at each other, pretending to catch the actual bouquet at an actual wedding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, ready?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
She turns around and throws the bouquet to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (catching it) I got it! Mine! (They both hug)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Congratulations!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay! My turn! My turn!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay! (Gets into position) Okay, ready?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (cocking her head from side to side in some pre-bouquet-catching ritual) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Phoebe turns and throws it on the couch.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (upset) That was a terrible throw!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm not gonna right to you! That's not real!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look at me! My big concern is what's real?! (Finally realises) Oh my God. We're really sad, aren't we?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I think we are.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This isn't even my dress.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, at least you didn't rent yours from a store called, "It's Not Too Late."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm changing out of this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: In like a half-hour?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, throw it straight this time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.
She throws it straight, and Monica makes a big deal about catching it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm getting married next!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yay!
Central Perk, Monica and Phoebe, back to reality, are sitting in normal clothes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I hate my regular clothes now! Y'know? I look down and-and I know that this isn't gonna be the most special day of my life.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. I mean it was kinda fun for a while, but didn't you start feeling silly?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I guess.
Monica crosses her legs and is still wearing the garter belt.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you're such a cheater!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hello! Little ones.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, is Joey gonna stop snoring?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yep! And! A beautiful woman agreed to go out with me. (They're stunned.) Joey wanted to ask her out, but uh, she picked me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, how'd that happen?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because I'm cooler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, seriously.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well she's, she's the kinda girl-Joey was unconscious.
Joey enters, wearing a mouth guard like boxers wear.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (muffled by the mouth guard) Hey you guys! What's happening?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What is that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (muffled) Oh, they gave it to me at the sleep clinic, and it's gonna help me not to snore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, are you asleep right now, Joe? 'Cause I don't think you have to wear it unless you are!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (takes out the mouth guard) I know I don't have too! It tastes good. (Puts it back in.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Plus, you look cool.
Joey totally agrees with this statement and kicks his feet up.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is putting away the wedding dress, finally.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering from her bedroom) Well, I just called Joshua...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, how did it go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I did my best to convince him that I'm not some crazy girl who is dying to get married-I'm just going through a hard time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What did he say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well uh, his answering machine was very understanding. Ugh. I feel blue.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, sweetie! (Goes to comfort her.) Hey, I bet you anything that he's gonna call you again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, maybe, but I don't think I even care. I don't think he's the one I'm sad about. Y'know, I know that I said that I am totally okay with Ross getting married, but as it turns out, I don't think I'm handling it all that well.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, maybe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I-I am just trying to figure out why.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Any luck?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, yeah, y'know how Ross and I were on again, off again, on again, off again? I guess I just figured that somewhere down the road, we would be on again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Again. Y'know what? I think we all did.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! (She jumps up and throws Emily's wedding dress into Rachel's room.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, I got us some reservations for Sunday night, okay? How about, Ernie's at 9 o'clock?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well, you uh, better make it for three.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, see I-I don't know if we're gonna be hungry at three.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Three people. Joshua's not gonna be there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, well, I think, I think he broke up with me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Noo. Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, apparently he scares easy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, Rachel, I'm-I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's okay. Sometimes, things don't work out the way you'd thought they would.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come here.
They hug.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (breaking the hug) Oh, hey, don't you have to go pick up Emily?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! I got my girls.
He leaves.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ugh. (She goes over and lays her head on Phoebe's lap.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looks at Monica) Hey, y'know what might cheer you up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?
[Time lapse, all three girls are now wearing wedding dresses, eating popcorn, drinking beer, and watching TV.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know, I gotta tell ya, this really does put in a better mood.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I wish there was a job where I could wear this all the time. (Pause) Maybe someday, there will be.
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh God! He's gonna come by and borrow some candles for his big date!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay! (She goes to answer the door.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no, Rachel, don't get it! He can't see us!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, yeah! The groom cannot see the bride!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm not gonna marry Chandler!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not after this!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you guys, just relax. (She goes over to open the door, and as she does, she says.) I doooo. (Sees that it's Joshua, not Chandler that knocked on the door.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, wait, Joshua! Joshua! (Pause) (Comes back inside) Yeah, well, that oughta do it.
Closing Credits

Chandler's bedroom, he is sleeping with Marjorie. All of the sudden, Marjorie starts talking in her sleep, awakening Chandler. After a little bit, she quiets back down, and Chandler tries to get back to sleep. There's a short pause until she starts screaming, causing Chandler to scream with her. She quickly calms down. This all wakes up Joey, who comes over wearing the mouth guard, opens the top half of Chandler's door, and starts to complain about the noise.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (muffled by the mouth guard) Dude! I am trying to sleep! (Shrugs to say, "What's up with that?")
End
